**Multimodal Psychophysiological Assessment Reveals Gastric but Not Cardiac Interoception Deficits in Disorders of Gut-Brain Interaction.** — Psychophysiology (2025). PMID: 41367122. DOI: 10.1111/psyp.70207. Score: 67. Disorders of Gut-Brain Interaction (DGBI) are characterized by persistent digestive symptoms in the absence of objectively detectable abnormalities. Visceral hypersensitivity and interoceptive dysfunction are central features of DGBI, yet the specific interoceptive impairments associated with these conditions remain underexplored.
